skill.name: Azure Blob Storage SDK for Rust
skill.description: A community-maintained Rust SDK for Azure Blob Storage. Provides idiomatic Rust bindings for uploading, downloading, and managing blob data in Azure Storage accounts.
skill.summary: Rust SDK for Azure Blob Storage operations
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only documentation skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files and found 0 security issues. The skill provides guidance on using the Azure Blob Storage Rust SDK - a legitimate cloud storage library. No dangerous patterns, no network access, no filesystem operations, and no external commands are present. Risk assessment: SAFE.

content.user_title: Use Azure Blob Storage with Rust
content.value_statement: This skill helps developers integrate Azure Blob Storage into Rust applications by providing guidance on SDK usage, common operations, and best practices for cloud blob management.
actual_capabilities: Generate Rust code for Azure Blob Storage operations
Explain blob upload and download patterns
Show container and blob management workflows
Help with error handling and retry strategies
limitations: This skill provides guidance only - it does not execute Azure operations
Requires valid Azure Storage credentials to use in production
Does not cover Azure Data Lake or advanced analytics features
May not reflect latest Azure SDK updates

use_cases.title: Cloud Backup Solution
use_cases.description: Implement automated backup to Azure Blob Storage for application data and logs

use_cases.title: Media Asset Storage
use_cases.description: Store and serve images, videos, and documents in cloud-native blob storage

use_cases.title: Data Lake Ingestion
use_cases.description: Stream large datasets into Azure for analytics and machine learning pipelines

prompt_templates.title: Basic Blob Upload
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to upload a file to Azure Blob Storage using the Rust SDK
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with simple file upload

prompt_templates.title: Download with Streaming
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I download a blob from Azure Storage and stream it in Rust?
prompt_templates.scenario: Efficiently reading blob data without loading entire file

prompt_templates.title: Container Management
prompt_templates.prompt: Create example code for creating, listing, and deleting blob containers

prompt_templates.title: SAS Token Auth
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I authenticate to Azure Blob Storage using Shared Access Signature (SAS) tokens?
prompt_templates.scenario: Secure authenticated access without connection strings

output_examples.input: How do I upload a file to Azure Blob Storage?

output_examples.output:
Azure Blob Storage Rust SDK supports multiple authentication methods:

1. Connection String - Quick setup for development
2. SAS Token - Fine-grained access control
3. Azure Identity - Managed identity for Azure services
4. OAuth 2.0 - Enterprise authentication

For production, prefer Azure Identity or SAS tokens over connection strings.

best_practices: Use Azure Identity for production authentication instead of storing connection strings
Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for transient failures
Use block blobs for large files and leverage parallel uploads
Enable logging and diagnostics to monitor blob operations
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode connection strings in source code - use environment variables
Avoid loading entire large blobs into memory - use streaming instead
Do not ignore error handling for network operations
Avoid using account name and key authentication in production

faq.question: What is Azure Blob Storage?
faq.answer: Azure Blob Storage is Microsoft's cloud object storage solution for storing massive amounts of unstructured data like text, images, videos, and documents.

faq.question: Is this skill official Microsoft code?
faq.answer: No, this is a community skill providing guidance. The actual SDK is maintained by the Azure team at github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-rust.

faq.question: Do I need an Azure account to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need an Azure Storage account to execute the code examples. You can create a free account at azure.com for testing.

faq.question: What Rust version is required?
faq.answer: The Azure Blob Storage SDK requires Rust 1.56 or later with async support via tokio.

faq.question: Can this skill handle large files?
faq.answer: Yes, the SDK supports block blobs for large files up to 4.75 TB and provides chunked upload capabilities.

faq.question: Is the data encrypted at rest?
faq.answer: Yes, Azure Storage encrypts all data at rest by default using Microsoft-managed keys. You can also use customer-managed keys for additional control.
